commit.message: Add `ResourceMark` RAII to guard allocation in lookup function

Count logical processors accurately using new PDH API perf counter

The code for enumerating logical processors in os_perf_windows.cpp uses
the Windows PDH API "Processor" performance counter, which unlike the
newer "Processor Information" performance counter, supports enumerating
processors in just the _first_ processor group, thus limiting the total
processor count to at most 64.  On systems with more than 64 cores, the
assertion in os_perf_windows.cpp checks whether this count reported by
the PDH API matches the one reported by `os::processor_count()`, causing
an assertion failure in FastDebug builds, resulting in a crash.

The key change in this patch is to switch to the "Processor Information"
counter, if available, otherwise fall back to the old "Processor"
counter.  There is a small difference in the old versus the new output;
specifically that the new output includes summary (total) information
for _each_ processor group whereas the old output had only one summary
line, since it measured processors in just the first group.

The PDH API also has a caveat that is particularly relevant to the new
Processor Information counter.  The `PdhEnumObjectItems()` API call
requires locale-specific counter names, unless the counter name is one
among the so-called base system counters.  Since the Processor
Information counter is new, it is not among the base system counters and
thus we need to use the locale-specific name.  To obtain this
locale-specific name, we first lookup the index of the Processor
Information counter (using registry keys) before performing a reverse
lookup of the locale-specific name using the index.  See KB 287159
article (also documented in the existing source file) for more
information about localization of performance counter names.
